steps.title: Identify the relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The second sentence doesn't contradict or pivot away—it confirms Langley's pioneering role by providing evidence: his manuscripts contain the first documented uses of 2,000+ English words. The transition must signal confirmation or elaboration.

steps.title: Select the confirmation signal
steps.content:
“Indeed” confirms the first sentence with supporting evidence. “However,” “That being said,” and “Besides” signal contradiction, concession, or addition respectively—none of which fits a sentence providing evidence.

This completion makes the logical relationship between the sentences clear.

That confirms D is correct.
